title: Lithococca tenella
taxonomy: Lithococca tenella is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae, which is a large and diverse family of plants commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This family consists of around 489 genera and over 5,000 species, including many plants that are native to tropical and subtropical regions. Lithococca tenella is further classified as a member of the genus Lithococca, which is a small genus of flowering plants found primarily in North America. The genus Lithococca is part of the subfamily Rauvoloideae, which is a group of flowering plants that are often characterized by their hairy or woolly stems and leaves. Within the family Apocynaceae, Lithococca tenella is a relatively rare and lesser-known species.

In traditional Chinese medicine, Lithococca tenella is used to treat fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. intro: Lithospermum tenellum, also known as Lithococca tenella, is a perennial herb belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae). It is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in the treatment of various conditions such as skin issues, wounds, and infections.
